heroSubtitle: Combine multiple PDF documents into one file in seconds. Fast, easy, and completely free — no signup required.
dragHint: Drag and drop your files here or click to browse
featuresSubtitle: Combining PDFs shouldn't be complicated. We made it as simple as possible — no hidden settings, no gotchas.
feature1Desc: Upload your PDFs, drag them into the order you want, click merge. That's literally it. No complicated settings or confusing options to figure out.
feature2Desc: Text stays sharp, images stay clear, links keep working, formatting stays perfect. We combine the files without touching the quality of anything inside them.
feature3Desc: Even if you're merging 20 big files, it only takes a few seconds. Upload, arrange, merge, download. The whole thing is done before you finish your coffee.
feature4Desc: Everything is encrypted in transit. We process on secure servers and delete your files after an hour. Nobody can access your documents except you.
feature5Desc: Phone, tablet, laptop, desktop. Windows, Mac, Android, iPhone. If you've got a browser, you can merge PDFs. No app needed.
feature6Desc: No trial that expires, no premium features locked away, no hidden charges. This is the whole tool, completely free, forever.
stepsSubtitle: Merge your PDFs in under a minute
step1Desc: Select at least 2 PDF files. You can grab them all at once or add them one by one — whichever is easier.
step2Desc: Drag the files around to put them in whatever order you want. The merged PDF will match this sequence exactly.
step3Desc: Hit the merge button, wait a couple seconds, then download your combined PDF. Done.
useCasesTitle: When You Need to Merge PDFs
useCasesSubtitle: Instead of sending five separate files, send one clean document. People appreciate it.
work1: Combine invoices from the whole month into one tidy file
work2: Send complete proposals with all supporting documents attached
work3: Put together training manuals from separate sections
work4: Bundle contracts and agreements with their amendments
personal1: Submit job applications with resume, cover letter, and references together
personal2: Keep all your tax documents and receipts in one organized file
personal3: Combine travel confirmations and tickets before your trip
personal4: Put together study materials and notes for easier reference
faq1Question: How many PDFs can I merge at once?
faq1Answer: Up to 20 files in one go, and each file can be up to 100 MB. Need to merge more? Just do it in batches — merge 20, then merge those results with the next batch.
faq2Question: Will the quality get worse when I combine them?
faq2Answer: Nope. Everything stays exactly as it was. Text looks the same, images are still sharp, formatting doesn't change. We just stick the files together without messing with the content.
faq3Question: Can I change the order before merging?
faq3Answer: Yeah, after you upload you'll see all your files laid out. Just drag them around to put them in whatever order you want. The final PDF will match that order.
faq4Question: Is this safe to use with private documents?
faq4Answer: Everything is encrypted when you upload. We process files on secure servers and delete both your originals and the merged file after an hour. Nobody can see your documents.
faq5Question: Do I need to install anything?
faq5Answer: No. Works right in your browser on any device. Phone, tablet, laptop, whatever. No downloads, no apps, no software to manage.
seoP1: Multiple PDF files are a pain to deal with. You've got to send them all separately, keep track of which one is which, and hope the person on the other end opens them in the right order. One merged file solves all of that at once.
seoP2: Think about sending a project proposal. Without merging, you're attaching the main proposal, then the budget as a separate PDF, then the timeline, then maybe some references. That's four files minimum. With merging? One file. Everything's in order, nothing gets lost, looks way more professional.
seoP3: Office workers combine monthly reports. Students merge research papers with citations. Freelancers bundle invoices with contracts. Real estate agents put together listing documents. Lawyers assemble case files. Anyone who deals with multiple PDFs regularly ends up needing to merge them at some point.
ctaSubtitle: Join thousands using SmallPDF.us every day. Fast, free, no signup needed.

download.privacyDesc: Your files are encrypted with 256-bit SSL during transfer and automatically deleted from our servers within 1 hour. We never access or store your document content.
